title: festuca tenuifolia var. paludosa
intro: Festuca tenuifolia var. paludosa, also known as Marsh Fescue, is a type of grass native to wetland areas in Asia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, known for its medicinal properties such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Marsh Fescue include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. Marsh Fescue can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions.
